title: Jain communities
overview: Overview of the social, religious, and demographic structure of the Jain community in India and abroad.

major_sects.key_features: Male monks renounce clothing, prevalent in Southern & Central India, women must be reborn as men for moksha
Monks and nuns wear white robes, large canon of scriptures, women can achieve moksha in this life, dominant in Western India

demographics.wealth: Wealthiest religious community in India (70% in highest wealth quintile)

social_structure.caste_system: Jains follow regional Jāti (caste) system despite philosophical rejection of Varna
social_structure.key_practices: Strict endogamy (marriage within caste), caste panchayats, occupational traditions in trade and business

economic_ethical_links: Ahimsa → Avoided farming and military professions
Aparigraha (non-attachment) → Strong tradition of philanthropy and temple patronage
diaspora: Significant communities in USA, Europe, East Africa, Japan (over 5,000 families converted)
SUMMARY:
The Jain community is a small but highly influential minority (0.4% of India) known for exceptional literacy, 
wealth, and strong adherence to Ahimsa. It is organized into a four-fold sangha (monastics + laypeople) and divided mainly 
into Digambara and Svetambara sects with further sub-sects. Social life revolves around regional castes (Jāti), while economic
success stems from ethical principles that channeled them into trade and business. The community maintains a remarkable
tradition of philanthropy, animal welfare, and temple building.
